summary.intent: Optimize the `saveGitOidMapping` call in the indexer to avoid redundant DB reads on every checkpoint pass at scale.
summary.outcome: Refactored `indexAllCheckpointsV2` to pre-fetch a `Map<checkpointId, gitOid>` once before the loop, then skip or upsert in `saveGitOidMapping` based on whether the exact (checkpointId, gitOid) pair already exists — handling rebase scenarios where the same checkpoint may remap to a new OID.

repo: The `CheckpointIdGitOidJoin` model uses `gitOid` as `@id`, meaning a checkpoint can be remapped to a new OID after a rebase — so presence-only caching is insufficient.
The indexer has two key functions: `indexAllCheckpointsV2` (orchestrator) and `indexCheckpointV2` (per-checkpoint), with `saveGitOidMapping` called inside the per-checkpoint function.
Gossamer project lives at /Users/stephanostsoucas/gossamer with Prisma ORM and TypeScript.

code.finding: `indexAllCheckpointsV2` now pre-fetches all existing (checkpointId -> gitOid) mappings into a Map<string, string> before the loop, passes it into `indexCheckpointV2` as an optional parameter, and `saveGitOidMapping` owns the skip/upsert logic by comparing the current resolved OID against the cached value.

workflow: Build verification with `npm run build` was used after edits to confirm TypeScript compilation success before finishing.
Iterative refinement: initial approach (Set-based skip) was corrected mid-session when the user identified the rebase edge case, leading to a more robust Map-based solution.

friction: Initial optimization using a `Set<checkpointId>` was logically incomplete — the rebase scenario (same checkpointId, new gitOid) was caught by the user rather than anticipated upfront.
open_items: The in-memory Map is populated before the loop but new mappings created during the pass are added to it — verify this stays consistent if indexing runs concurrently.
The upsert behavior in `saveGitOidMapping` after a rebase (changing an existing mapping) should be tested explicitly with a real rebase scenario.
Consider whether the pre-fetch query should be scoped (e.g., by repository or branch) to avoid loading all mappings when only a subset is being indexed.
